title: artocarpus montanus
intro: Artocarpus montanus, also known as the Fijian breadfruit, is a species of breadfruit tree native to the Pacific Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which help to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Artocarpus montanus include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating digestive problems, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. Herbal preparations of Artocarpus montanus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for internal use, and it can also be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
